{"timestamp":"2026-04-12T19:36:28.381Z","hook_event_name":"PreToolUse","session_id":"43a3e303-cac4-43fc-960b-af933008dd23","transcript_path":"c:\\Users\\Dario\\AppData\\Roaming\\Code\\User\\workspaceStorage\\364529e75fa950547ab3ff1c137a9ec2\\GitHub.copilot-chat\\transcripts\\43a3e303-cac4-43fc-960b-af933008dd23.jsonl","tool_name":"create_file","tool_input":{"filePath":"c:\\Users\\Dario\\Desktop\\faks\\6.semestar\\ASP.NET\\projekt\\.github\\agents\\ux-mobile-phone-ui.agent.md","content":"---\ndescription: \"Use when designing or implementing UX/UI for mobile phone service and sales web apps, especially dark-theme, card-based, non-standard layouts in ASP.NET MVC/Razor views.\"\nname: \"UX Mobile Phone UI\"\ntools: [read, edit, search]\nargument-hint: \"Describe the page or flow to design, target users, required data fields, and any brand/style constraints.\"\nuser-invocable: true\n---\nYou are a specialized UX/UI implementation agent for mobile phone service and sales system web applications.\n\nYour job is to design and implement clean, efficient, professional, and distinct interfaces that avoid generic templates.\n\n## Scope\n- Work on ASP.NET MVC/Razor UI code: .cshtml views, layout files, and related CSS/JS.\n- Optimize user flow for common business tasks:\n  - Service request intake\n  - Repair status tracking\n  - Product browsing and sales checkout\n  - Customer and order overviews\n- Prioritize readability, hierarchy, and operational speed for both desktop and mobile users.\n\n## Design Direction\n- Default to a dark visual theme unless the user explicitly asks otherwise.\n- Use card-based layout patterns to group related content and actions.\n- Produce non-standard, intentional visual design; do not output default Bootstrap-looking screens.\n- Define and use CSS variables for palette, spacing, and elevation for consistency.\n\n## Constraints\n- DO NOT redesign backend/domain models unless the user requests it.\n- DO NOT introduce unnecessary dependencies for simple styling tasks.\n- DO NOT output purely decorative UI that harms task completion speed.\n- ONLY make UI decisions that improve clarity, discoverability, and efficiency.\n\n## Approach\n1. Read existing layout, styles, and relevant views to preserve project conventions where needed.\n2. Propose or apply a page structure with explicit sections, card groups, and primary actions.\n3. Implement responsive behavior for mobile and desktop, including spacing, typography, and card stacking.\n4. Add purposeful visual identity with strong hierarchy, contrast, and restrained motion.\n5. Validate consistency across views and avoid regressions to existing functionality.\n\n## Implementation Checklist\n- Clear visual hierarchy and scannable sections\n- Dark theme with accessible contrast\n- Card-based grouping for data and actions\n- Non-generic styling (not default Bootstrap appearance)\n- Mobile-first responsiveness\n- Efficient task flow for service and sales scenarios\n\n## Output Format\nWhen you complete a task, return:\n1. What UX problem was solved.\n2. Which files were created or changed.\n3. Key design decisions (theme, cards, hierarchy, responsiveness).\n4. Any assumptions and quick follow-up options.\n"},"tool_use_id":"call_LVndGYsdFiWJ6RDcBCucGWjX__vscode-1776020593014","cwd":"c:\\Users\\Dario\\Desktop\\faks\\6.semestar\\ASP.NET\\projekt"}

Dictionary<int, SparePart>();\n+            var technicianMap = new Dictionary<int, Technician>();\n+\n+            foreach (var customer in customers)\n+            {\n+                foreach (var phone in customer.Phones)\n+                {\n+                    phone.Customer = customer;\n+                    phone.CustomerId = customer.Id;\n+                    phoneMap[phone.Id] = phone;\n+\n+                    foreach (var repairJob in phone.RepairJobs)\n+                    {\n+                        repairJob.Phone = phone;\n+                        repairJob.PhoneId = phone.Id;\n+                        repairJobMap[repairJob.Id] = repairJob;\n+\n+                        if (repairJob.Technician != null)\n+                        {\n+ (!repairJob.Technician.RepairJobs.Any(rj => rj.Id == repairJob.Id))\n+                            {\n+                                repairJob.Technician.RepairJobs.Add(repairJob);\n+                            }\n+                        }\n+\n+                        foreach (var sparePart in repairJob.UsedParts)\n+                        {\n+                            sparePartMap[sparePart.Id] = sparePart;\n+                            if (!sparePart.RepairJobs.Any(rj => rj.Id == repairJob.Id))\n+                            {\n+                                sparePart.RepairJobs.Add(repairJob);\n+                            }\n+                        }\n+                    }\n+                }\n+\n+                foreach (var order in customer.Orders)\n+                {\n+                    order.Customer = customer;\n+                    order.CustomerId = customer.Id;\n+                    orderMap[order.Id] = order;\n+\n+                    foreach (var orderItem in order.OrderItems)\n+                    {\n+                        orderItem.Order = order;\n+                        orderItem.OrderId = order.Id;\n+\n+                        if (orderItem.Product != null)\n+                        {\n+                            orderItem.ProductId = orderItem.Product.Id;\n+                            productMap[orderItem.Product.Id] = orderItem.Product;\n+\n+                            if (!orderItem.Product.OrderItems.Any(oi => oi.Id == orderItem.Id))\n+                            {\n+                                orderItem.Product.OrderItems.Add(orderItem);\n+                            }\n+                        }\n+                    }\n+                }\n+            }\n+\n+            Phones = phoneMap.Values.OrderBy(p => p.Id).ToList();\n+            Orders = orderMap.Values.OrderBy(o => o.Id).ToList();\n+            Products = productMap.Values.OrderBy(p => p.Id).ToList();\n+            RepairJobs = repairJobMap.Values.OrderBy(rj => rj.Id).ToList();\n+            SpareParts = sparePartMap.Values.OrderBy(sp => sp.Id).ToList();\n+            Technicians = technicianMap.Values.OrderBy(t => t.Id).ToList();\n+        }\n+    }\n+}\n*** End                  repairJob.TechnicianId = repairJob.Technician.Id;\n+                        }\n+                    }\n+                }\n+\n+                foreach (var order in customer.Orders)\n+                {\n+                    order.Customer = customer;\n+                    order.CustomerId = customer.Id;\n+\n+                    foreach (var orderItem in order.OrderItems)\n+                    {\n+                        orderItem.Order = order;\n+                        orderItem.OrderId = order.Id;\n+\n+                        if (orderItem.Product != null)\n+                        {\n+                            orderItem.ProductId = orderItem.Product.Id;\n+                        }\n+                    }\n+                }\n+            }\n+\n+            Phones = customers\n+                .SelectMany(c => c.Phones)\n+                .OrderBy(p => p.Id)\n+                .ToList();\n+\n+            Orders = customers\n+ .OrderBy(rj => rj.Id)\n+                .ToList();\n+\n+            Products = Orders\n+                .SelectMany(o => o.OrderItems)\n+                .Where(oi => oi.Product != null)\n+                .Select(oi => oi.Product!)\n+                .GroupBy(p => p.Id)\n+                .Select(g => g.First())\n+                .OrderBy(p => p.Id)\n+                .ToList();\n+\n+            SpareParts = RepairJobs\n+                .SelectMany(rj => rj.UsedParts)\n+                .GroupBy(sp => sp.Id)\n+                .Select(g => g.First())\n+                .OrderBy(sp => sp.Id)\n+                .ToList();\n+\n+            Technicians = RepairJobs\n+                .Where(rj => rj.Technician != null)\n+                .Select(rj => rj.Technician!)\n+                .GroupBy(t => t.Id)\n+                .Select(g => g.First())\n+                .OrderBy(t => t.Id)\n+                .ToList();\n+\n+            foreach (var technician in Technicians)\n+            {\n+                technician.RepairJobs = RepairJobs\n+                    .Where(rj => rj.Technician?.Id == technician.Id)\n+                    .ToList();\n+            }\n+\n+            foreach (var sparePart in SpareParts)\n+            {\n+                sparePart.RepairJobs = RepairJobs\n+                    .Where(rj => rj.UsedParts.Any(sp => sp.Id == sparePart.Id))\n+                    .ToList();\n+  @{ void TimelineDot(int threshold, int current, string label, string icon, string align) {\n                                    bool isPassed = current >= threshold;\n                                    \n                                    // Make dot active if its threshold exactly matches the current progress (0=Pending, 50=InProgress, 100=Completed/Cancelled).\n                                    bool isActive = (threshold == 0 && current == 0) || \n                                                    (threshold == 50 && current == 50) || \n                                                    (threshold == 100 && current == 100);\n                                                    \n                                    // Change label/icon for the final dot if the job is cancelled.\n                                    if (threshold == 100 && statusStr == \"Cancelled\") {\n                                        label = \"Cancelled\";\n                                        icon = (threshold == 100) dotColor = \"success\";\n                                        else dotColor = \"primary\";\n                                    } else if (isPassed) {\n auto; z-index: 2; position: relative; transition: all 0.3s ease;\">\n                                            @Html.Raw(icon)\n                                        </div>\n
